title: Plan mode default-deny bypassed by unknown tools + MCP/plugin tool registration
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: Security and Access (5) / SOLID / OCP (3.2)

Location: src/session/state.rs:95-111 (PLAN_MODE_ALLOWED_TOOLS, PLAN_MODE_BLOCKED_TOOLS as const &[&str]).

Violation: The allow/block lists are hardcoded static string slices. Problems:

1. Drift: Every time a new tool is added to tools.rs (e.g. memory_save, chainlink, lsp, any MCP tool), the plan-mode allowlist must be manually updated. The memory_save tool writes to the DB — it is mutating — but would it be blocked? It's not in either list, so line 168 returns false (default-deny) — OK. But memory_search IS mutating via FTS rebuild triggers even on read? No, FTS is just reads. Still, the architectural coupling is bad: plan-mode policy lives in session/state.rs while tools are defined in tools.rs. When MCP loads a tool at runtime (mcp.rs) it never appears in either list, so default-deny applies — which is correct by luck but not by design.
2. Stringly-typed: tool_name: &str. A typo anywhere in the allowlist silently fails open for the typo'd name (never matches) or silently fails closed (never matches either), but there is no enforcement that the names correspond to real registered tools.
3. OCP violation: Adding a new safe tool requires editing a central list rather than extending the tool definition.
4. PLAN_MODE_BLOCKED_TOOLS is redundant with the default-deny at line 168 — if a tool is not in ALLOWED it is already blocked. BLOCKED exists only to prevent accidentally adding it to ALLOWED later, but the cleaner design is a single source of truth.

Mandated Refactor:
1. Move plan-mode policy ONTO the tool definition: ToolDefinition struct gains a plan_mode: PlanModePolicy enum { Allow, Deny, PlanFileOnly }.
2. is_tool_allowed_in_plan_mode dispatches based on the registered tool's policy. Unknown tools are PlanModePolicy::Deny by default at the registry layer.
3. MCP-loaded tools default to Deny unless their manifest declares plan_mode_safe: true AND the manifest is from a trusted source.
4. Delete PLAN_MODE_BLOCKED_TOOLS (redundant).
5. Add an integration test that iterates every registered tool and asserts it has an explicit plan_mode policy.
